scientific_justification.what_is_being_claimed: The observed linear Hall voltage response under an applied magnetic field can be interpreted as evidence of charge-carrier transport characterized by a well-defined Hall coefficient, without asserting a specific scattering or band-structure model.

linearity_axis.description: Linearity of Hall voltage with magnetic field

sign_axis.description: Sign of Hall voltage slope with respect to magnetic field

magnitude_axis.description: Order of magnitude of Hall voltage signal relative to measurement noise

l1_observed_coordinates.evidence: Hall voltage exhibits a linear dependence on magnetic field with a clearly resolvable negative slope at room temperature.

parameter_interpretations.interpretation_note: A negative Hall voltage slope suggests electron-dominated charge transport.
The finite Hall voltage magnitude allows an order-of-magnitude estimation of carrier density.

scientific_justification.important_note: This scientific justification does not assert microscopic transport mechanisms. It provides a conditional framework for interpreting Hall measurements in terms of carrier type and transport parameters only when the stated observational and measurement conditions are satisfied.
